_meta.description: Site-wide information - hours, prices, locations, contact details, historical figures, and America 250 program details
_meta.staffInstructions: This file contains all the basic information about Rocky Mount that appears throughout the website. Edit this file when admission prices change, contact info updates, or hours text needs adjustment. Always save a backup before editing. Changes go live automatically after saving.

fieldGuide.site: Basic site identity - name, tagline, historical dates. RARELY needs editing.
fieldGuide.location: Physical address, GPS coordinates, driving directions. Update only if address changes.
fieldGuide.contact: Phone, email, website, social media handles. Update when contact info changes.
fieldGuide.hours: Display text for hours shown on website. NOTE: This is DISPLAY ONLY - the actual open/closed logic comes from operatingSchedule.json
fieldGuide.admission: Current ticket prices by category. UPDATE ANNUALLY or when prices change.
fieldGuide.admissionIncludes: List of what's included with admission. Update if tour offerings change.
fieldGuide.whatToExpect: Tour details and accessibility info. Update if tour format changes.
fieldGuide.sisterSites: Nearby historic sites for cross-promotion. Rarely needs updates.
fieldGuide.nearbyAttractions: Local attractions for visitors. Update as needed.
fieldGuide.first250: America 250 founding program details. This is a one-time 2026 program.
fieldGuide.america250: 2026 commemoration details and Rocky Mount's historical role.
fieldGuide.historicalFigures: Key people in Rocky Mount history. Content rarely changes.

rules: Prices must be numbers, not text (12 not '$12')
Social handles are just the username, no @ symbol
Always test the site after making changes
commonMistakes: WRONG: 'price': '$12' - Don't include dollar sign, use number only
WRONG: Editing hours.regular days to change when we're open - Those are DISPLAY ONLY, actual logic is in operatingSchedule.json
WRONG: Forgetting the comma after a value - Every line except the last in a section needs a comma

_priceUpdateChecklist.description: Follow these steps when updating admission prices
steps: 6. Save file and verify site builds correctly
_priceUpdateChecklist.example: To change adult price from $12 to $15: change '"price": 12' to '"price": 15'

_aiRules.description: Rules for AI assistants editing this file
beforeEditing: ALWAYS verify price changes with user before committing
NEVER change contact phone/email without explicit permission
priceRules: Prices in this file are in DOLLARS (not cents)
Always confirm price changes with user
If updating prices, also update operatingSchedule.json
prohibited: Do NOT change site.name or site.shortName
Do NOT modify address without verification
Do NOT change hours.formatted without updating operatingSchedule.json

crossFileSync.hours: Display text only - actual logic in lib/siteHours.ts

_aiRules.whenUnsure: Ask the user rather than making assumptions about site operations

site.name: Rocky Mount State Historic Site
site.established_note: Farm established per Century Farms certification; William Cobb settled the property c. 1770

territorialCapital.description: First seat of government for the Southwest Territory

location.directions: Located off US-11E between Johnson City and Bristol. From I-26, take Exit 13 and follow signs to Rocky Mount.

hours._note: These are DISPLAY values only. The actual open/closed logic is calculated from data/config/operatingSchedule.json
hours.seasonNote: Season opens March 4, 2026 for America 250 commemoration
hours.tourNote: Tours depart every hour. Last tour at 4pm.
hours.note: Hours may vary for special events.

admission._note: Prices are numbers only (no dollar signs). These should match the tiers in operatingSchedule.json

groups.note: Groups of 10 or more, please call for rates

features: Guided living history tour with costumed interpreters
Walk through the historic Cobb House
Visit the detached kitchen, weaving cabin, and grounds
Learn about daily life in 1790s frontier Tennessee

accessibility.summary: Multiple tour options available for different accessibility needs

museumGallery.name: Museum Gallery Tour
museumGallery.description: Indoor exhibit tour with artifacts and displays

historicSiteTour.name: Historic Site Tour
historicSiteTour.description: Outdoor living history tour of the grounds and buildings
historicSiteTour.note: Please call ahead to discuss specific accessibility needs

sisterSites.name: Tipton-Haynes State Historic Site
sisterSites.description: 45-acre site with eleven restored buildings, limestone cave, and nature trails

sisterSites.name: Sycamore Shoals State Historic Park
sisterSites.description: Site of the 1775 Treaty and reconstructed Fort Watauga

sisterSites.name: Carter Mansion
sisterSites.description: Tennessee's oldest frame house (circa 1780), National Historic Landmark

sisterSites.name: Chester Inn State Historic Site
sisterSites.description: Oldest wood-frame building in Tennessee's oldest town, hosted three U.S. Presidents

sisterSites.name: Warriors' Path State Park
sisterSites.description: 970-acre park on Fort Patrick Henry Lake, named for the Great Indian Warpath

sisterSites.name: Holston Heritage Museum
sisterSites.description: Showcasing the historical and cultural heritage of the Holston River Valley region

nearbyAttractions.name: Downtown Jonesborough
nearbyAttractions.description: Tennessee's oldest town, home of the National Storytelling Festival

nearbyAttractions.name: Downtown Bristol
nearbyAttractions.description: Birthplace of Country Music, straddling the TN/VA state line

first250.description: Join 250 Tennesseans as founding participants in Rocky Mount's America 250 commemoration.
benefits: Name read aloud at July 4, 2026 ceremony

america250.rockymountRole: Rocky Mount served as the first seat of government for the Southwest Territory from 1790-1792, laying groundwork for Tennessee statehood.

historicalFigures.name: William Blount
historicalFigures.title: Governor of the Southwest Territory
historicalFigures.hook: Signed the U.S. Constitution. Appointed by George Washington. Made Rocky Mount his headquarters.
details: Appointed by President George Washington as territorial governor
One of the signers of the U.S. Constitution
Lived at Rocky Mount with the Cobb family during his tenure
Made Rocky Mount the first capital of the Southwest Territory
Established foundational laws and land policies for the frontier region

historicalFigures.name: William Cobb
historicalFigures.title: Original Settler
historicalFigures.hook: Staked his claim on this land six years before the Declaration of Independence was signed.
details: Established the homestead c. 1770 (current structures built 1826-1828 main house, 1829-1830 dining room by grandson Michael Massengill per dendrochronology)
Brought his family from North Carolina to establish the estate
He and his sons responded to John Sevier's call for volunteers in September 1780
Supplied the Overmountain Men with gunpowder, horses, blankets, and food for the Battle of King's Mountain
In 1796, transferred Rocky Mount to his son-in-law Hal Massengill (husband of daughter Penelope)
